name: Lolopo

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Lolopo (lolo1259).

notes: Skeleton. Lolopo (Central Yi) is a Loloish (Sino-Tibetan) language of Yunnan, China. Its primary script is the Yi syllabary; this entry maps a Latin-based transcription, covering base Latin consonants and vowels plus digraphs ch/sh/ts/ng/ny and aspirates kh/ph/th. The three lexical tones (low 21, mid 33, high 55) are not recovered by the romanization and are left unencoded, so an elevated PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Lolopo (lolo1259)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
